TranslationContext: This file contains Unit Names for the Dwarf Hold Faction.

Translations.Comment: Literal faction name. '堡垒' means fortress/stronghold, fitting for dwarven settlements.
Basic infantry unit. '战士' is the standard term for warrior in fantasy context.
Experienced infantry. '老兵' conveys seasoned, battle-hardened soldiers.
Workers/laborers. Dwarves are renowned for mining in fantasy lore.
Crossbow unit. '弩手' is the standard term for crossbowmen.
Gunpowder unit. '火枪手' means gunner/musketeer, fitting the early firearm aesthetic.
Ranged scouts. '游侠' means ranger/wanderer, conveying mobility and skill.
Artillery unit. Simple and direct term for heavy cannon.
Siege weapon. '弩炮' combines crossbow and cannon concepts, fitting a hybrid siege weapon.
Siege weapon. '投石机' is the traditional term for catapult/trebuchet.
Elite melee unit. '狂战士' conveys savage fury and combat prowess.
Elite unit, suggesting underground/deep origins. '深渊卫士' conveys guardian of the depths.
Specialized artillery, likely fire-based. '地狱' means inferno/hell, emphasizing its destructive power.
Unique unit. '飞行器' means flying machine/aircraft, maintaining the technological/steampunk vibe.
Leader unit, likely from worker ranks. '工头' means foreman/overseer, fitting for a dwarven leader.
Elite ranged leader. '队长' means captain/commander, maintaining consistency with ranger terminology.
Technical specialist. '工程师' means engineer, reflecting dwarven craftsmanship and technology.
